items.q: Can you stop a foreclosure in Florida?
items.a: Yes. In Florida, options exist to halt foreclosure — including evaluating a cash-offer path that can close in 7–21 days. We tailor the approach to your situation and help you understand the timeline and your rights under Florida law.

items.q: What is the fastest way to stop a foreclosure?
items.a: The fastest path is typically a cash sale that can close in days, avoiding repairs and agent commissions. A cash buyer can make an offer quickly and close before the foreclosure sale date, giving you certainty and immediate relief.

items.q: What is the 120-day rule for foreclosure?
items.a: Foreclosure timelines vary. The 120-day period is a common reference window in which lender actions may occur after missed payments, but specific timelines depend on your lender, loan type, and state law. Consult a local attorney or HUD counselor for guidance specific to your case.

items.q: How many mortgage payments can you miss before foreclosure in Florida?
items.a: There is no single fixed number. Missed payments trigger lender actions, but options like counseling, modifications, or cash offers can alter timing. Generally, foreclosure proceedings may begin after several months of missed payments — acting early gives you more options.

items.q: Is my cash offer calculation free?
items.a: Yes — completely free and no-obligation. We evaluate your property in person, review local comps, and give you a written cash offer. You're under no pressure to accept and there are no costs to you for requesting the evaluation.

items.q: Are there any fees or commissions?
items.a: None. We don't charge commissions, agent fees, or closing costs. The number in your offer is the number you receive at closing — no deductions, no surprises.

items.q: What kinds of homes do you buy?
items.a: Any condition — foreclosures, fixer-uppers, inherited homes, vacant properties, homes with tenants, fire-damaged homes, and more. We buy as-is in St Petersburg, Clearwater, Largo, and across the Greater Tampa Bay area.

items.q: What government programs can help stop foreclosure?
items.a: Federal and state foreclosure-prevention resources include HUD-certified counselors, mortgage forbearance and modification programs, and the HOPE NOW Alliance. We can point you to official programs and counselors to help you explore all options — not just a cash sale.

items.q: Will I work with a real person?
items.a: Yes — always. John Gardepe or a member of our local St Pete team will be your point of contact throughout the entire process. No call centers, no bots, no runarounds.

finalCta.description: Facing foreclosure in St Petersburg? Get a no-obligation cash offer within 24 hours. No repairs. No commissions. Close on your timeline.

comparison.lede: When facing foreclosure, speed and certainty matter more than maximizing list price. Here's how the paths compare.

infoBlocks.title: When Might Bankruptcy Help Stop Foreclosure?
infoBlocks.body: When a bankruptcy petition is filed, an automatic stay halts most foreclosure actions. But it introduces legal complexity, costs, and long timelines.

chapters.title: Chapter 7
chapters.body: Liquidation. Foreclosure may resume after stay is lifted if you can't make payments. Fresh start, but may not save your home.

chapters.title: Chapter 13
chapters.body: Reorganization with a 3–5 year repayment plan. Can keep your home if you complete the plan successfully.

infoBlocks.footer: A cash sale provides a different path: sell the property, pay off the mortgage, and move forward without a multi-year repayment plan or ongoing legal proceedings. Consult a local attorney, then compare with your cash offer.

infoBlocks.title: Government Programs & Resources to Stop Foreclosure

list.title: HUD-certified housing counselors
list.body: Free or low-cost counseling to review your options, negotiate with lenders, and explore loan modifications. Find a counselor at hud.gov/counseling.

list.title: Mortgage forbearance & modifications
list.body: Contact your loan servicer to discuss temporary payment reductions, forbearance plans, or loan modifications.

list.title: HOPE NOW Alliance
list.body: Partnership between government agencies and mortgage servicers to prevent foreclosures.

list.title: Florida Housing Finance Corporation
list.body: State programs that may offer assistance or refinancing options for eligible borrowers.

callout.title: Foreclosure imminent?
callout.body: These programs can provide relief but often require time and documentation. If you need a fast solution, a cash offer can close before the auction date — giving you certainty and immediate relief.

items.q: Do you buy inherited houses as-is with probate considerations?
items.a: Yes. We buy inherited houses as-is — no repairs, no cleaning required — and we can help with probate-related steps to keep the sale moving. We work alongside your probate attorney throughout.

items.q: How fast can you close?
items.a: Closing can happen in as little as 7 days after you accept our offer, subject to title clearance. If probate is still in progress, we coordinate the closing timing with your probate attorney to ensure everything is in order.

items.q: Are there commissions or fees?
items.a: No real estate commissions or fees — ever. The cash offer you receive is exactly the amount you will receive at closing. We cover all standard closing costs.

items.q: Is probate required to sell inherited property?
items.a: Probate may be involved depending on how the property was owned. We can guide you through the process and help start the sale preparation in parallel, reducing your overall timeline from inheritance to cash in hand.

items.q: What documents are needed to start?
items.a: Most sellers start with proof of ownership (or documentation of the estate), a valid ID, and any available probate documents. We'll guide you through gathering everything needed for a smooth closing.

items.q: Do you handle title clearance?
items.a: Yes — we help with title clearance as part of the sale. We coordinate with trusted local Pinellas County title companies to ensure a clean, compliant transfer of ownership at closing.

items.q: Can I sell inherited property without repairs?
items.a: Yes — we buy in any condition. You don't need to repair, clean, stage, or even empty the property before closing. We handle everything after the sale.

items.q: Are there taxes I should plan for?
items.a: Tax questions are best answered by a qualified CPA or tax attorney. Generally, Florida has no state inheritance tax, and the IRS "stepped-up basis" often minimizes capital gains if you sell shortly after inheriting. We recommend consulting a Florida tax professional for your specific situation.

items.q: How do I start the process?
items.a: Call us at (727) 477-8998 or fill out our online form to receive a free, no-obligation cash offer. We'll walk you through every step from your first call to closing. Reach out to our team →

items.q: Do you have to sell your house in a divorce in Florida?
items.a: No — not automatically. In Florida, you can mutually agree to sell, one spouse can buy out the other, or the court can order a sale. Selling is often the cleanest path to a clear financial break, but it's not always required.

items.q: Can one spouse sell the house without the other's consent in Florida?
items.a: In Florida, a sale typically requires mutual agreement or a court order. If one spouse blocks the sale, the court may compel a sale through equitable distribution proceedings under Florida Statute 61.075.

items.q: Can a court force the sale even if one spouse objects?
items.a: Yes. A court can compel a sale if it serves equitable distribution goals and the legal threshold for partition is met. Once ordered, both parties are legally required to cooperate with the sale process.

items.q: What happens to the mortgage when a divorce happens in Florida?
items.a: The mortgage remains the responsibility of whoever's name is on it, regardless of a divorce decree. Selling the home with a cash buyer pays off the mortgage at closing, providing a clean break for both parties without the risk of one spouse defaulting on a shared obligation.

items.q: How quickly can I sell my Florida home during divorce?
items.a: Speed depends on court timelines and whether a cash sale is pursued. We can close in as little as 7 days once both parties agree and title is clear — providing certainty and reducing stress during a difficult time.

items.q: Is it better to sell or keep the house in a divorce?
items.a: There's no one-size-fits-all answer. Selling provides liquidity and a clean break. Keeping the home may make sense if one spouse can afford the mortgage and wants to maintain stability for children. A tax advisor and family law attorney can help you weigh the specific equity and tax implications.

items.q: Is it possible to use a cash offer when there is disagreement between spouses?
items.a: Yes. Cash offers provide a neutral, court-acceptable path when disputes exist. The offer terms must be reviewed by both parties and approved by the court if necessary. We work directly with attorneys to ensure the offer meets all legal requirements.

items.q: What are the typical timelines from filing to cash-close in Florida?
items.a: With mutual agreement and a cash buyer, you can close in 7–21 days. If court orders are required, add several weeks for the legal process. Traditional listings with financing add 60–90 days or more. Court schedules and title checks are the primary variables.

items.q: What local resources can I reference for divorce-related property matters in St. Petersburg?
items.a: Contact the Pinellas County Clerk of the Circuit Court, Pinellas County Court, and local title companies. These offices handle court records, filings, title documents, and property dispute proceedings in Pinellas County.

items.q: What areas do you serve in the Tampa Bay region?
items.a: We serve St. Petersburg, Clearwater, Largo, Gulfport, Pinellas Park, Palm Harbor, Tampa, Manatee, Palmetto, Sarasota, Pasco, Hudson, New Port Richey, Dunedin, and surrounding Tampa Bay communities.

finalCta.description: Navigating a home sale during divorce is hard enough. We buy houses as-is across St. Petersburg and Tampa Bay — no fees, no commissions, no repairs. Close in as little as 7 days and divide proceeds on your timeline.

items.q: Is it legal to sell a Florida rental with tenants in place?
items.a: Yes — Florida explicitly allows tenant-occupied property sales. The lease follows the property to the new owner, and tenants retain their occupancy rights throughout the transaction. The sale can proceed while tenants continue to live there normally.

items.q: What is the process to sell a rental property with tenants in Florida?
items.a: Contact us for a no-obligation property assessment. We review your lease terms, tenant situation, and property condition. Within 24–48 hours, we present a fair cash offer. Once you accept, we handle all tenant communication, coordinate deposit transfers, and manage closing. We can close in as little as 7 days — you don't deal with showings, repairs, or tenant coordination.

items.q: Can I sell a Florida rental property with tenants for cash?
items.a: Yes — cash offers are available for tenant-occupied properties, often with a faster, smoother closing than traditional sales. Cash buyers don't require financing contingencies, appraisals, or lender-mandated vacant possession. We buy as-is with tenants in place.

items.q: What happens to the lease when the property is sold in Florida?
items.a: The current lease generally survives the sale, and the buyer takes over as landlord under the existing lease terms. Florida law protects tenant occupancy rights — the new owner inherits all landlord responsibilities and the tenant continues living there through the remainder of their lease period.

items.q: What is cash-for-keys and does it speed up the closing?
items.a: Cash-for-keys is a voluntary agreement where a tenant vacates early in exchange for a relocation payment. We may offer tenants this incentive when vacant possession accelerates the close or benefits all parties. This voluntary arrangement benefits everyone — you close faster, tenants receive compensation, and the buyer gets clear possession.

items.q: What happens to the security deposits at closing?
items.a: Security deposits must either transfer to the buyer (with written notice to tenants) or return to tenants with proper accounting. Florida statutes require written notice identifying the new owner and confirming the deposit amount. We coordinate this transfer seamlessly during closing to protect you from post-sale liability.

items.q: What documents should I prepare for a tenant-occupied Florida sale?
items.a: Prepare: current lease agreements, rent payment history, security deposit amounts, any outstanding maintenance requests or disputes, and any existing tenant notices. We guide you through the full documentation process — you don't need to figure it out alone.

items.q: Do buyers care about tenancy when purchasing a rental with tenants in place?
items.a: Investor buyers often view tenant occupancy positively — it means immediate rental income, no lease-up period, and proven tenant demand. We evaluate lease quality and payment history, but we buy properties with tenants regardless of these factors because we're equipped to handle any situation.

items.q: Why choose We Buy St Pete Houses for selling tenant-occupied Florida property?
items.a: Local expertise, cash offers, no commissions, and a fast, transparent closing process. We specialize in tenant-occupied properties across St. Petersburg and Tampa Bay. You'll work directly with our local team, receive a fair cash offer within 24–48 hours, and close on your timeline — often in as little as 7 days. We handle all tenant coordination so you can move forward stress-free.

finalCta.description: Done being a landlord? We buy tenant-occupied rental properties across St. Petersburg and Tampa Bay — as-is, for cash, with no commissions or repairs. We handle all tenant coordination. Close in as little as 7 days.

items.q: Can you sell a property with a lien on it in Florida?
items.a: Yes. In Florida, you can sell a liened property, and we can help you through a fast, cash-close process. The lien is paid off from the sale proceeds at closing — you don't need to resolve it before the sale or bring cash to the table.

items.q: Can you sell a house with a lien on it in Florida for cash?
items.a: Yes. We offer cash for liened homes and aim to close quickly with no repairs. Our as-is purchase model handles liens directly at closing — cash buyers like us don't require financing contingencies or lender-mandated clear title before closing.

items.q: What is the process to sell a home with liens in Florida?
items.a: Submit property details, receive a cash offer within 24 hours, confirm the offer, coordinate lien payoff at closing, and close on your timeline. We handle all lien verification and payoff steps through the title company — you don't manage any of it directly.

items.q: How long does a lien stay on your property in Florida?
items.a: Lien duration varies by type. Judgment liens typically last 10 years and can be renewed. Tax liens vary based on the agency. HOA and mechanic's liens follow Florida's lien enforcement statutes. Regardless of how long a lien has been in place, it can be resolved as part of a cash closing — we handle the payoff process at the table.

items.q: What happens to lien payoff from the proceeds when selling to a cash buyer?
items.a: At closing, the lien is paid off from the sale proceeds, and the remainder goes to you. The title company coordinates the payoff directly with each lien holder, confirms payoff amounts, and issues disbursements on closing day. You receive your net proceeds after all liens are satisfied.

items.q: Can a lien be resolved before closing in Florida?
items.a: Yes — you can resolve liens before closing, but it's not required when selling to us. We coordinate all lien payoffs as part of the closing process, which is simpler and eliminates the need to pay liens out of pocket in advance. Most sellers prefer to let the closing proceeds handle it.

items.q: Do you charge any fees or commissions?
items.a: No. We buy as-is with no agent fees or commissions and offer a cash close. We cover typical closing costs. The number in your offer is what you receive — no deductions, no hidden fees, no surprises.

items.q: What documents will I need to start the process?
items.a: Bring proof of ownership (deed or title) and any lien documentation you have. We'll guide you through the rest. Basic property information is all we need to get started — our team handles lien research and verification from there.

items.q: Is selling a liened home legally complicated?
items.a: Not overly, especially with a buyer experienced in Florida lien transactions. We handle the lien payoffs and closing steps clearly to reduce risk, working with title companies experienced in Florida lien resolution. The complexity is manageable — we've done it dozens of times across Tampa Bay.

finalCta.description: Selling a house with a lien in Florida doesn't have to be stressful. We buy as-is, handle the lien payoff at closing, and can close in as little as 7 days. No agent fees. No repairs. No pressure.

A cash buyer eliminates this obstacle — we purchase directly without lender requirements, making flood zone properties fully sellable.

items.q: Is selling a flooded house in St Petersburg feasible?
items.a: Yes. We buy flood-damaged houses in St Petersburg for cash, AS-IS, with fast closes. You don't need to repair anything or wait for traditional buyer financing. We evaluate your property in its current condition and make a fair cash offer.

items.q: How long does closing take on a flood-damaged home?
items.a: Cash offers can close quickly. We've helped sellers close in as little as a week to two weeks when conditions align. The exact timeline depends on your readiness and situation — we prioritize speed and flexibility, and we work around your schedule.

items.q: Do I need to repair before selling?
items.a: No. We buy AS-IS, so repairs are not required for our cash offers. Whether your home has water stains, structural concerns, mold, or significant interior damage, we purchase it in its current state. You avoid the cost and hassle of repairs entirely.

items.q: What documents are needed to start the sale?
items.a: We'll gather property info and basic identification, and we'll guide you through every document you need. Typically, this includes proof of ownership, property details, and a few standard disclosures. Our team walks you through each step to make the process straightforward.

items.q: Do you offer cash and are there fees?
items.a: Yes, we offer cash for AS-IS properties. There are no commissions, and the process is transparent. The cash offer we make is what you receive — no hidden fees, no realtor commissions, no closing costs passed to you.

items.q: Is this service available in surrounding areas of St Pete?
items.a: We serve St Pete and nearby neighborhoods within Pinellas County, including Shore Acres, Pinellas Point, Old Northeast, Clearwater, Gulfport, Largo, and Pinellas Park. If your property is in or near St Petersburg, we're interested.

items.q: What is the fastest way to start the sale today?
items.a: Contact us to get a quick, no-obligation review and a cash-offer timeline that fits you. Reach out by phone, email, or our online form — share a few details about your flooded property and we'll respond promptly with next steps.

items.q: How can I verify the trust signals shown for your service?
items.a: We provide verifiable testimonials and transparent timelines, and you can request references. Our local reputation in St Petersburg matters to us — we're committed to demonstrating trustworthiness through clear communication and proven outcomes in this community.

items.q: Can I sell a fire-damaged house as-is?
items.a: Yes — you can sell as-is for a fast cash offer with no required repairs. We buy homes in their current condition, fire damage and all. You don't need to clean, repair, or make the property presentable before we evaluate it.

items.q: What to do with a fire-damaged house?
items.a: You have a few options: repair and list traditionally (expensive and time-consuming), file an insurance claim and wait for settlement, or sell as-is to a cash buyer for a fast, certain exit. If speed and simplicity are priorities, a cash sale to a local buyer gives you a clear path forward without the complexity of contractor bids and insurance negotiations.

items.q: What documents are required to sell a fire-damaged house?
items.a: Typically proof of ownership (deed or title), government-issued ID, and property tax information. Fire damage documentation is optional but helpful. We'll guide you through every document step to make the process as smooth as possible.

items.q: How long does a cash sale take?
items.a: Many sellers close within 7 days after accepting a cash offer. The timeline is flexible — we work on your schedule. If you need more time to arrange your move or handle other details, we accommodate that too.

items.q: Are there fees or commissions with a cash sale?
items.a: There are no real estate commissions or typical closing fees in our cash-offer path. You keep more of the sale proceeds. The offer you receive is what you walk away with, minus any standard title and recording fees we typically cover.

items.q: What is a cash offer, exactly?
items.a: A cash offer is a no-financing-required proposal from a buyer ready to pay with cash. It removes the uncertainty of loan approvals and speeds up closing — there's no lender requiring vacant possession, no appraisal, and no financing contingency that can cause the deal to fall through.

items.q: Do you buy homes with liens or in probate?
items.a: Yes — we evaluate distressed properties including those with liens or probate considerations. We can often work through these issues as part of the sale, coordinating with title professionals and estate attorneys to ensure a clean, compliant transaction.

items.q: Do you operate outside St Petersburg?
items.a: We serve St Pete and the greater Tampa Bay area, including Clearwater, Tampa, Largo, Sarasota, Pinellas Park, Palm Harbor, Dunedin, and surrounding communities across Pinellas, Hillsborough, Manatee, Pasco, and Sarasota counties.

items.q: What kind of properties do you buy?
items.a: We buy fire-damaged, vacant, inherited, and distressed properties. We also purchase homes with liens, problem tenants, upside-down mortgages, and other issues that scare off traditional buyers. If your home has challenges, we're interested.

items.q: Why should I choose We Buy St Pete Houses over a traditional sale?
items.a: Because we offer speed, simplicity, no repairs, and no real estate commissions. You get a fair cash offer and close on your timeline — no showings, no waiting for a qualified buyer, no financing fall-through risk, and no repair demands from lenders or buyers.

hero.subheadline: Hurricane, tropical storm, or flood damage doesn't have to mean months of repairs and uncertainty. We buy storm-damaged homes across Florida as-is for cash — no repairs, no commissions, close in as little as 7 days.

items.q: Can you sell a house with storm damage?
items.a: Yes — cash buyers will buy storm-damaged homes as-is. You don't need to make repairs or wait for insurance claims to settle. Cash buyers evaluate your property in its current condition and make an offer that accounts for the damage. This option is ideal if you want to avoid the time and expense of repairs.

items.q: Do I need to disclose flood damage when selling a storm-damaged home in Florida?
items.a: Yes. Florida law requires sellers to disclose known material defects, including flood damage. Failing to disclose can lead to legal issues after the sale. When you work with a cash buyer, we guide you through the disclosure process to ensure compliance and protect both parties.

items.q: How soon can I close on a storm-damaged Florida house with a cash buyer?
items.a: Closes can occur in as little as 7 days with a cash offer. The exact timeline depends on your schedule and how quickly you can gather necessary documents. Unlike traditional sales, there are no financing delays or inspection contingencies, so the process moves quickly.

items.q: What costs are involved when selling to a cash buyer?
items.a: Typically no seller fees beyond the agreed-upon purchase price — no commissions. We cover closing costs. You walk away with the full offer amount minus any agreed-upon adjustments. There are no agent commissions, staging fees, or repair costs.

items.q: What is the process to sell a storm-damaged home in Florida?
items.a: Contact us, review your offer, sign, and close — often within days. The process starts with a simple phone call or online form. We ask about your property's condition and timeline, then present a cash offer within 24–48 hours. Once you accept, we coordinate with a title company to finalize the paperwork and close on your schedule.

items.q: Should I file my insurance claim first, or sell first?
items.a: It depends on your situation. If you plan to repair and list traditionally, file your claim first. If you want to sell as-is for cash, you can proceed without waiting for insurance. Some sellers file claims and then sell before repairs are completed, using the payout to offset the reduced sale price. Discuss your options with us during evaluation — there's no obligation.

items.q: What should I know about selling hurricane-damaged Florida properties?
items.a: You must disclose known damage, but you don't need to repair it. Cash buyers handle properties with roof damage, water intrusion, mold, and structural issues. The process is faster and more certain than a traditional sale — ideal for homeowners facing urgency or financial pressure.

items.q: Are there fast cash offers for storm-damaged Florida houses?
items.a: Yes. We specialize in storm-damaged properties and provide offers within 24–48 hours. These offers account for the damage and allow you to close quickly, often in as little as 7 days. If speed and certainty are priorities, a cash offer is your best option.

items.q: Is there a hurricane grant in Florida worth applying for?
items.a: Grants exist through state agencies — check with the Florida Division of Emergency Management (1-800-342-3557) for current programs. Some provide assistance for repairs or temporary housing, but eligibility and funding vary significantly. A cash offer may provide faster financial relief than waiting for grant approval or insurance settlement.

finalCta.description: Hurricane, tropical storm, or flood damage doesn't have to mean months of repairs and uncertainty. We buy storm-damaged homes across Florida as-is for cash — no repairs, no commissions, close in as little as 7 days.

items.q: Is it worth getting a cash offer for my house?
items.a: Yes — cash offers provide speed and certainty with fewer repair requirements. You avoid the risk of buyer financing falling through and can close on your own timeline without waiting months for a traditional deal to settle.

items.q: What is a typical cash offer on an as-is home?
items.a: Offers vary by condition, location, and current market conditions. Cash buyers account for repair costs and holding expenses when making an offer. The trade-off for a potentially lower offer is complete certainty, speed, and no out-of-pocket repair costs on your end.

items.q: How long does it take to close a cash sale?
items.a: We can close in as little as 7 days with a cash offer, depending on your readiness and the title search. Traditional sales can take 30–60 days or longer, often with unexpected delays tied to lender timelines or inspection negotiations.

items.q: Are cash home buyers legit in St Petersburg?
items.a: Yes — but due diligence matters. Work with a local, reputable cash buyer who has verifiable contact information, references, and a physical presence in the area. We Buy St Pete Houses is a local LLC with a proven track record in Pinellas County. Always review any contract with an attorney before signing.

items.q: Can I sell as-is in Florida?
items.a: Yes — selling as-is is completely legal in Florida. You're not required to make repairs before closing. Cash buyers purchase properties in any condition, which is precisely why as-is sales and cash buyers are such a natural fit.

items.q: What is the process of selling a home for cash?
items.a: The process is five steps: (1) Contact us and discuss your situation. (2) Receive a written cash offer. (3) Accept and choose your closing date. (4) Close without making repairs. (5) Move out and receive your funds at closing. We handle the paperwork and coordinate with the title company throughout.

items.q: What are the closing costs on a cash deal in Florida?
items.a: Closing costs typically include title search, title insurance, and recording fees. Cash deals often minimize or eliminate realtor commissions, reducing your overall out-of-pocket costs compared to a traditional sale. We cover our share of standard closing costs as part of our offer.

items.q: Can a house be sold while in probate in Florida?
items.a: Yes — but probate steps may extend the closing timeline. We've worked through many probate situations in the St Petersburg area and can connect you with local probate attorneys to clarify requirements. Consult a qualified attorney to understand the specific process for your estate.

items.q: What is the hardest month to sell a house?
items.a: Traditional sales can slow significantly during certain seasons, but cash offers prioritize your speed over market seasonality. If you need to sell quickly, a cash buyer removes timing concerns entirely — we buy year-round and on your schedule.

finalCta.description: We buy houses for cash in St Petersburg in any condition — no repairs, no agent commissions, no hidden fees. Get a fair, no-obligation cash offer and close in as little as 7 days, on your schedule.

items.q: Can I sell my house as-is in Florida?
items.a: Yes. Selling a house as-is in Florida is a common and legal option. We buy homes as-is for cash, handling properties in any condition without requiring you to make repairs. We close quickly and transparently, often within 7 days.

items.q: How fast can I sell my house as-is in Florida?
items.a: We can close in as little as 7 days, depending on your schedule and title clearance. Most as-is cash sales complete within 7 to 14 days — far faster than traditional listings which average 30 to 60+ days.

items.q: What is the Florida as-is contract?
items.a: The Florida as-is contract is a standard agreement (using FAR/BAR forms) stating the property is sold in its current condition with no repair obligations for the seller. The buyer accepts the home as it stands. We explain every clause before you sign so there are no surprises.

items.q: Are there hidden fees or costs in a cash sale?
items.a: No hidden fees. There are no agent commissions in a direct cash sale. Standard closing costs such as title and recording fees may apply, but we explain all costs upfront so you know exactly what to expect before you sign anything.

items.q: What steps are involved to close a Florida cash sale?
items.a: The process: 1) Contact us with property details, 2) Receive a cash offer within 24 hours, 3) Review and accept if it works for you, 4) Sign the Florida as-is contract, 5) We coordinate title work with a local title company, 6) Move out on your schedule, 7) Close and receive payment. As fast as 7 days total.

items.q: Do I need a lawyer to sell my home as-is in Florida?
items.a: A lawyer is not required in Florida for a for-sale-by-owner or cash transaction, but legal review can help — especially with disclosures and contract terms. We guide you through the process and ensure you understand each step before committing to anything.

items.q: What happens if the home has HOA issues or liens?
items.a: We address HOA dues and liens during our evaluation. Cash buyers work directly with title companies to resolve these issues before closing — simplifying your responsibilities and ensuring a smooth transaction without delays.

items.q: Is it better to sell as-is or fix the house up first?
items.a: It depends on your priorities. If you need to move quickly, avoid repair costs, and value certainty, selling as-is for cash makes sense. If maximizing sale price is your only goal and you have time and money to invest in repairs, a traditional listing may yield more. For most time-pressured sellers, the speed and simplicity of a cash sale outweigh the price difference.

items.q: How can I compare a cash offer vs. listing with repairs in Florida?
items.a: Compare total costs, timelines, and net proceeds. Cash offers close quickly with no repair expenses or agent commissions. Traditional listings may yield a higher gross price but require repair investment (often $10,000–$50,000+), 5–6% agent commission, and 30–60+ days of uncertainty. Net proceeds are often closer than they appear.

items.q: What is the typical timeline to close a Florida cash sale?
items.a: Most Florida cash sales close within 7 to 14 days. We can close in as little as 7 days if you're ready to move quickly. The timeline depends on your schedule, title clearance, and any HOA or lien coordination needed.

items.q: What local market factors in St. Pete affect as-is cash offers?
items.a: Market demand, neighborhood dynamics, property condition, and proximity to desirable amenities all influence cash offers in St. Pete. Areas with strong buyer demand and waterfront or urban access command higher offers. We analyze recent sales and local trends to ensure your offer is fair and competitive.

items.q: Is Florida a good state to sell a house as-is?
items.a: Yes. Florida has a well-established as-is sale framework using FAR/BAR contracts, a predictable disclosure process, and a strong investor market — particularly in the Tampa Bay region. Local cash buyer demand is high, making as-is sales practical and fast for Florida homeowners.

finalCta.description: No repairs. No showings. No agent commissions. We buy houses in St. Pete and across Florida as-is for cash — closing in as little as 7 days. You sell the home exactly as it stands and move on with certainty.

label: Cash home buyers
metaTitle: Cash Home Buyers St Petersburg | We Buy St Pete Houses
metaDescription: Sell your St Petersburg home fast for cash. No fees, no repairs, close in as little as 7 days. Get a fair cash offer from We Buy St Pete Houses today.

hero.titleTail: — fast, fair offers with no fees.
hero.formIntro: A local team of specialists — buying homes across Pinellas County at fair prices. No agents, no repairs, no surprises.

process.lede: Our quick steps: No bidding wars, no repair headaches, no complex listings — certain results you can count on.

steps.title: Tell us about the home
steps.body: Share your property details via our form or by phone. We listen to your specific situation first.

steps.title: We visit, in person
steps.body: A local team member schedules a walkthrough at a time that's convenient for you. No hassle.

steps.title: Receive a written offer
steps.body: We present a fair, no-obligation cash offer with transparent terms — no hidden fees, ever.

steps.title: Close on your timeline
steps.body: You choose the closing date. We handle the paperwork. You get cash at closing.

areas.lede: We focus on the neighborhoods we know personally — streets we've bought houses on for over a decade.

items.q: Can I sell my home AS-IS to a cash buyer? +
items.a: Yes. We buy houses in any condition and pay cash, with no repairs needed. Whether your home needs major work or just minor updates, we'll make you a fair offer based on its current state.

items.q: How long does closing take with a cash buyer? +
items.a: We can close in as little as 7 days, depending on your situation. If you need more time, we're flexible — we work on your schedule.

items.q: Are there any fees or commissions when selling to cash buyers? +
items.a: No. There are no real estate commissions or hidden fees when selling to us. The cash offer we present is the amount you receive at closing.

items.q: What is a cash home buyer in St. Petersburg? +
items.a: A cash home buyer is a person or company that purchases real estate with available funds rather than requiring mortgage financing. This allows for faster closings, no contingencies, and the ability to buy properties in any condition — including as-is.

items.q: What is the typical process to sell to a cash buyer? +
items.a: Submit your property details, receive a cash offer, and close quickly if you accept. Our three-step process is designed to be simple and transparent — contact us, receive your offer, and close on your schedule.

items.q: Do you buy inherited or probate properties? +
items.a: Yes, we consider inherited and probate properties. We understand these situations can be stressful and time-sensitive, and we're here to help you navigate the sale smoothly.

items.q: How do you determine the cash offer amount? +
items.a: We base offers on your property's condition, local market data for your specific St. Petersburg neighborhood, and our straightforward business model. Each property is evaluated individually, and we walk you through our reasoning so you understand every factor.

items.q: Cash home buyers vs. traditional buyers — which is faster? +
items.a: Cash home buyers are significantly faster. Traditional buyer transactions typically take 30–60+ days due to mortgage approvals, appraisals, inspections, and negotiations. A cash sale can close in as little as 7 days with no financing contingencies and no repair negotiations.

items.q: Can I compare your offer to a traditional buyer before deciding? +
items.a: Yes — we encourage it. Our offer is completely no-obligation, so you can take your time, compare your options, and decide what's best for you. We're confident our offer will be competitive when you factor in the savings on repairs, commissions, and holding costs.

items.q: Which areas do you serve near St. Petersburg? +
items.a: We serve St. Petersburg and the broader Tampa Bay area, including Clearwater, Gulfport, Largo, Pinellas Park, Palm Harbor, Dunedin, Tampa, Sarasota, New Port Richey, Hudson, and surrounding communities.
